body_txt: The janitor: the job that virtually every establishment needs but very few are interested in doing. Someone is expected to keep things tidy and well-maintained, but this is usually a laborious, unsexy position with mediocre pay, and thus it tends to attract very particular kinds of people, with the most common perception of a janitor (or a "custodian", or "caretaker", or "porter", etc.) being that of a surly, stone-faced stoic, usually of a minority ethnic group , who does a demeaning job cleaning up everyone else's messes out of necessity. As a result of this stereotype, it becomes meaningful when a character working the position breaks it, revealing that they're not only interesting but perhaps...more colorful. Perhaps the janitor, rather than being professional and minding their own business all the time, is easygoing and has a sense of humor. Instead of keeping to themselves, perhaps they're social and chatty, willing to strike up a conversation with other characters while on the job. Given that being a janitor is often a solitary task where interaction with others is infrequent, perhaps they have some interesting hobbies, habits, and idiosyncrasies when alone, potentially with the little attention they receive from management and the public enabling them to engage in some mischief. If a janitor is sufficiently well-developed, there's usually an interesting story as to why they're in their position, such as them being much more experienced and skilled than they let on but having fallen on hard times or perhaps because they genuinely like their job . Not to be confused with Almighty Janitor , which applies to all professions about characters who are low in rank but have surprisingly high power (though a literal janitor who is both Quirky and Almighty is not out of the question). Also may or may not be the subject of an Oblivious Janitor Cut .
